validationCommand: python -c "import torch; assert not torch.cuda.is_available()" # then run the node and confirm no AttributeError on torch.backends.cuda
knownIssues: ModelPatchTorchSettings.patch() unconditionally reads/writes torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_fp16_accumulation, which doesn't exist on the XPU (or any non-CUDA) backend — crashes on load for any workflow that includes this node on an XPU target, even though the node has nothing to do with attention/kernel specifics that would justify a CUDA-only limitation.

workarounds.action: Apply patches/kjnodes-xpu-fp16-guard.patch: return the cloned model unchanged (skip the CUDA-specific fp16-accumulation toggle entirely) when torch.cuda.is_available() is false.
workarounds.rationale: Minimal, correctness-only guard — the fp16-accumulation setting has no XPU equivalent to apply, so the safest behavior is a no-op rather than a crash.
workarounds.tradeoff: None on XPU (the setting simply isn't applied, matching the fact that it never had an XPU implementation to begin with).

validationEvidence: Discovered as an uncommitted local-only fix on local-xpu's disk (never committed to git) while consolidating this package onto the shared NFS custom_nodes tree during llm-scaler-omni bundle adoption; recovered, committed, and propagated to the shared copy (commits d2234da locally / 43df8e6 on the shared tree) on 2026-07-22.
retireCondition: kijai/ComfyUI-KJNodes adds a torch.cuda.is_available() (or torch.xpu-aware) guard around this setting upstream.
